The paper "Incidence and distribution of Sweet potato feathery mottle virus and Sweet potato chlorotic stunt virus in Uganda" (2016) by Tugume et al. (or similar) describes the use of Nitrocellulose Membrane Enzyme-Linked Immunosorbent Assay (NCM-ELISA). The two most commonly used chemicals (enzymes) in this diagnostic method are Alkaline Phosphatase and Horseradish Peroxidase. Alkaline Phosphatase has the EC number 3.1.3.1, and Horseradish Peroxidase has the EC number 1.11.1.7. Alphabetizing the chemicals (Alkaline Phosphatase, then Horseradish Peroxidase) results in the sequence of their EC numbers.

<final_answer>3.1.3.1; 1.11.1.7</final_answer>
